translation.en: The sky today is very blue.

notes_md:
**xanh** means *green* (a color). It’s an adjective and comes **after** the noun it describes.

It describes things by color and can act like a verb in simple sentences (no “to be” needed).  

Very common extra meaning: **blue** (Vietnamese doesn’t clearly separate green/blue like English; context tells you which).  
It can also mean **unripe/young** (for fruit) or **pale/sickly** in some contexts.  

Memory tip: think of **xanh lá cây** (leaf green) to lock in the core color meaning.
exampleSentencesTranslated: I like to wear a green shirt to work on Mondays.
The trees in my garden always keep their leaves green all year round.
She bought a green bicycle for her younger brother for his birthday.
notes:
xanh means green (a color). It’s an adjective and comes after the noun it describes.

It describes things by color and can act like a verb in simple sentences (no “to be” needed).  

Very common extra meaning: blue (Vietnamese doesn’t clearly separate green/blue like English; context tells you which).  
It can also mean unripe/young (for fruit) or pale/sickly in some contexts.  

Memory tip: think of xanh lá cây (leaf green) to lock in the core color meaning.

example.es: El cielo está muy azul hoy.
example.vi: Bầu trời hôm nay rất xanh.
example.en: The sky is very blue today.

definitions.en: A color that resembles the color of the sky or sea.
definitions.vi: Một màu sắc giống như màu của bầu trời hoặc biển.
definitions.es: Un color que se asemeja al color del cielo o del mar.

exampleSentences: Anh thích mặc áo màu xanh khi đi làm vào thứ Hai.
Cây trong vườn nhà em luôn giữ lá xanh quanh năm.
Chị mua một chiếc xe đạp xanh cho em trai nhân dịp sinh nhật.
